other_observers: Tony Leukering, M. Ob.; Tony & I found the bird
description: Large, heavy billed, bulky gull. From a distance appears dark winged, pale headed. Upon closer inspection the bill reveals a small almost white tip followed by a clearly defined dark third or so and the remaining two thirds of the bill is pink. Head mostly white, with slight dark smudging behind the eye. The scapulars are mottled gray and black. The remaining wing feathers are mostly checkered dark grayish brown with white highlights, a white tertial crescent, and black primaries. The bird does not appear especially long winged. The bird was noticeably larger than nearby Herring Gulls.Behaviors: The bird was associating with other gulls that were harassing a raft of Common Mergansers.Call: not heardPlumage: 2nd basic
